hard_stops.label: cost_gate
hard_stops.logic: Every data pull must show the projected cost and receive explicit user confirmation before execution.

hard_stops.label: audit_anchor_required
hard_stops.logic: Every data pull must produce a pull-receipt anchored to PLAT-008 Audit Trail. No receipt means no pull.

hard_stops.label: no_investment_advice
hard_stops.logic: Output text must not state that a parcel is a good investment. Output feasibility signals + named blockers only.

hard_stops.label: cross_worker_timeout
hard_stops.logic: Cross-worker calls (W-002, PLAT-008, Vault DTC, Land Use AI Attorney, Permit Processors) must time out gracefully and surface a named blocker. No silent failures.

hard_stops.label: input_validation
hard_stops.logic: Reject malformed inputs: fictional ZIP codes, malformed APNs, search radii greater than 5 miles, polygon inputs exceeding 10 square miles. Surface a specific input-error message.

hard_stops.label: fair_housing_pattern
hard_stops.logic: Refuse searches that suggest redlining, steering, or any pattern correlated with protected-class avoidance. Surface a regulatory note and decline to execute. RAAS Tier 2 Fair Housing module governs detection.

hard_stops.label: map_required
hard_stops.logic: Every Opportunities tab render must include a map marker layer alongside the table. No table-only views in v1.

hard_stops.label: visual_before_verdict
hard_stops.logic: Street View OR satellite imagery must render for the parcel before any feasibility verdict is shown. Operator must have the option to see the parcel before reading the verdict. Non-negotiable because it changes which verdicts a human will trust.

hard_stops.label: apn_retirement
hard_stops.logic: Parcels with retired APNs must be removed from the ranked opportunity list and surfaced as a named blocker in the Feasibility tab.

soft_flags.label: persona_lock
soft_flags.logic: Persona must not be silently upgraded. A First-Timer who runs 20 sessions stays a First-Timer until they explicitly self-upgrade in Settings.

soft_flags.label: green_verdict_conditions
soft_flags.logic: Green verdict requires: clean title chain, fresh assessor data, fresh AVM, active APN, no coastal commission overlay, no historic district overlay. Any failure caps verdict at Yellow with named blocker.

soft_flags.label: stale_assessor_data
soft_flags.logic: Assessor data older than 180 days (6 months) triggers a staleness warning. Verdict capped at Yellow. Parcel still appears in ranked list.

soft_flags.label: owner_mismatch_flag
soft_flags.logic: Owner-of-record mismatch flags the parcel Yellow with OWNER_MISMATCH blocker. Parcel remains in the ranked list.

soft_flags.label: list_cap
soft_flags.logic: Ranked list capped at 50 parcels per session. Default is 10. User may expand up to 50 but must confirm cost projection before expansion.

soft_flags.label: avm_freshness
soft_flags.logic: AVM data older than 30 days triggers a staleness warning. Verdict capped at Yellow. Separate threshold from assessor data (RULE-05).

soft_flags.label: youtube_discovery_cap
soft_flags.logic: YouTube video discovery returns at most 3 results per parcel. Each result must meet filter: duration > 60s, view count > 100, uploaded within last 36 months.

soft_flags.label: view_premium_detection
soft_flags.logic: When same-floorplan units in the same building/stack show price variance greater than 15%, render the unit stack with per-floor view imagery and attach VIEW_PREMIUM_SUSPECTED caveat. Never silently treat divergent comps as equivalent. The ATTOM same-floorplan-different-floor anomaly is explained by view, not by data noise.
